channel.title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
channel.description: Breaking news and world news from France 24 on Business, Sports, Culture. Video news. News from the US, Europe, Asia Pacific, Africa, Middle East, America

item.title: South Africa reach knockout stage for first time with win over South Korea
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: World Cup 2026: South Africa reach knockout stage for first time with win over South Korea
item.description: South Africa reached the World Cup knockout stage for the first time on Wednesday with a 1-0 victory over South Korea, completing a remarkable recovery after losing their opener. Thapelo Maseko scored the decisive second-half goal as Bafana Bafana secured second place in Group A behind Mexico after drawing with the Czech Republic.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Venezuelan president declares state of emergency after earthquakes
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Venezuelan president declares state of emergency after earthquakes
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Middle East live: Iran warns against attempts to cross Strait of Hormuz without authorisation
item.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guards said Thursday they would take "appropriate measures" against vessels attempting to sail through the Strait of Hormuz without Tehran's authorisation. The warning comes as top US diplomat Marco Rubio visits Bahrain after promising Gulf allies that Washington will protect their interests as it seeks to hammer out a final settlement to end the war with Iran. Follow our liveblog for the latest developments.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Vinicius Junior scores twice as Brazil beat Scotland to reach last 32
item.description: Vinicius Junior scored twice as Brazil eased to a 3-0 win over Scotland on Wednesday, sealing top spot in Group C and a place in the World Cup last 32. Matheus Cunha added a third, while Neymar made his first international appearance in more than two-and-a-half years after returning from injury.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Venezuela declares state of emergency after back-to-back earthquakes leave dozens dead
item.description: Venezuela's interim leader declared a state of emergency on Wednesday after two powerful earthquakes and more than 20 aftershocks struck the country, damaging infrastructure and forcing the closure of Caracas' main airport. Acting President Delcy Rodriguez said the 7.2- and 7.5-magnitude quakes killed at least 32 people and injured more than 700.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Morocco complete comeback against Haiti in Group C clash
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Morocco complete comeback against Haiti in Group C clash
item.description: Morocco beat Haiti 4-2 in a dramatic Group C encounter in Atlanta, in a match that was level at half-time after a breathless first period. Achraf Hakimi and Ismaël Saibari restored parity before Soufiane Rahimi and Gessime Yassine struck late to seal victory, as Haiti were ultimately undone by Morocco’s late pressure and attacking depth.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Brazil beat Scotland to win World Cup Group C
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Rubio assures Gulf allies US will protect interests in Iran peace talks
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio was in Bahrain on Thursday to assure Gulf allies that Washington would protect their interests as it pursues a final settlement with Iran. Earlier, President Donald Trump said talks were progressing well, while requesting nearly $88 billion in supplemental funding for the Pentagon after Congress passed a resolution urging an end to hostilities.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Modigliani nude sets European auction record at London art sale
item.description: A London auction of artworks from billionaire Joe Lewis's private collection raised $392.6 million on Wednesday, led by an Amedeo Modigliani nude portrait that set a European auction record. The first sale featured 48 works by artists including Pablo Picasso, René Magritte and Gustav Klimt. Lewis, former majority owner of Tottenham Hotspur, transferred his stake to a family trust in 2022.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Trump says NATO allies ‘let down’ US by not backing Iran war
item.description: Trump told NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte on Wednesday he was disappointed that alliance members did not back the US military campaign against Iran, saying Washington had "demolished" Iran without needing assistance. Rutte defended allies, noting that 4,000-5,000 US aircraft operated from bases in Europe during the conflict.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Haiti - Morocco: five-goal thriller with late Atlas Lions surge
item.description: Morocco faced Haiti in a decisive final Group C match and secured a 4-2 victory in a chaotic, end-to-end encounter. However, with Brazil beating Scotland 3-0 and holding a superior goal difference, the Seleção finish top of the group, leaving Morocco to qualify in second place. In a thrilling match that saw both sides trade goals, the Atlas Lions eventually pulled away thanks to their late attacking depth. Relive the match to see how it unfolded.
item.dc:creator: Mehdi BOUZOUINA

item.title: France experiences its hottest day since measurements began in 1947
item.description: France on Wednesday experienced its hottest day since measurements began in 1947, the national weather agency said, breaking a record set just a day earlier. Around 44 million of the nearly 67 million people in France are currently under the highest red alert level for heat. FRANCE 24's Luke Shrago reports.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: US in 'panic' at prospect of Gulf States going it alone, analyst says
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio promised the United States' Gulf allies that Washington would protect their interests as it seeks to hammer out a final settlement of the Middle East war in talks with Iran. Speaking with FRANCE 24's Sharon Gaffney, independent journalist and author of the newsletter Badlands Borzou Daraghai says that the White House is experiencing "panic" at the prospect of Gulf countries creating an alternative security arrangement in the region that could undermine the Abraham accords.
item.dc:creator: Sharon GAFFNEY

item.title: World Cup 2026: Bosnia boost knockout hopes after dominant win over Qatar
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Bosnia boost knockout hopes after dominant win over Qatar
item.description: Bosnia and Herzegovina edge closer to knockout after dominant win over Qatar 3-1 in Seattle. Kerim Alajbegovic opened the scoring with a stunning strike, before an own goal from Mahmoud Abunada doubled their advantage. Despite Hasan Al Haydos pulling one back for Qatar, Ermin Mahmic sealed Bosnia's victory in the 80th minute.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Switzerland edge past Canada in Group B decider
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Switzerland edge past Canada in Group B decider
item.description: Switzerland claimed a narrow 2-1 victory over Canada at BC Place in Vancouver to secure top spot in Group B. Ruben Vargas and Johan Manzambi scored for the Swiss, while Promise David's late effort proved only a consolation as Canada fell short in a tightly contested encounter between two evenly matched sides.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Power outages and melting roads: Heatwave strains French infrastructure
item.description: Power outages, train delays, and melting roads - the heatwave is straining France's infrastructure. Electricity provider EDF says it has had to reduce its nuclear power output, but that it still has enough capacity to meet demand. Also in the show - the UN's International Maritime Organization starts evacuating some of the 11,000 sailors stranded in the Strait of Hormuz, and Europe's defence industry prepares for a boost with the IPO of Franco-German tankmaker KNDS.
item.dc:creator: Kate MOODY

item.title: Europe leaders reaffirm unity on defence ahead of Ankara NATO summit
item.description: European leaders met Wednesday in Berlin ahead of next month's NATO summit in Ankarai to discuss European defence and security guarantees for Ukraine, following tensions with the United States and among the main European members.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Did Qatar's prime minister 'snub' JD Vance? Doha, Washington deny claim
item.description: A viral clip from the first round of US-Iran peace talks in Switzerland fuelled online speculation that US Vice President JD Vance was snubbed by Qatar’s Prime Minister, after users claimed the video shows him "ignore" Vance in favour of greeting Pakistan’s Prime Minister. The Qatari PM though, dismissed the claims as “unfounded,” saying he and Vance had already spent hours together during the talks. The White House also rejected the narrative, pointing the finger at Iran for the rumours.
item.dc:creator: Vedika BAHL
